name: button-group
title: Button group
description: Visually joins related Commons <Button>s into one connected segment — a segmented control of independent actions or a split button (a primary action attached to a menu trigger). Styles the passed buttons rather than rendering its own: collapses the shared seam, rounds only the outer corners, and lifts the focused child so its focus ring is never clipped.
useWhen: A cluster of related, independently-actionable buttons that reads as one control, e.g. Cut / Copy / Paste
A split button — a primary action button attached to a menu-trigger button (chevron)
Toolbar-style action clusters where the seam between adjacent buttons should visually collapse
avoidWhen: Mutually exclusive choices where only one option is active at a time (use a radio-group or toggle group instead — ButtonGroup does not manage selection state or roving tabindex)
A single standalone button (no grouping benefit)
Unrelated actions that happen to sit near each other (grouping implies a relationship; do not use it purely for spacing)

keyboard: Normal Tab order between buttons — this is NOT a toggle/radio group, so there is no roving tabindex
Every button keeps its own focus-visible ring; the focused (or focus-within) button is lifted with z-index so its ring overlays adjacent buttons instead of being clipped by the collapsed seam
Each button keeps its own native activation contract (Enter/Space) and its own accessible name — grouping is purely visual
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default — each child keeps its own Commons <Button> size (min-h-11 or larger); the group does not shrink targets
notes: role="group" conveys nothing to a screen reader on its own — an `aria-label` (or `aria-labelledby`) is required on <ButtonGroup>; a dev-only console warning fires once if neither is present
The group renders the buttons it is given verbatim (data-slot="button-group"); it does not clone, wrap, or alter each button's own accessible name or role
Outer corner rounding uses logical radii (rounded-s-sm / rounded-e-sm) in the horizontal orientation, so first/last rounding flips correctly under dir="rtl"
Each Commons <Button> keeps a real border in every state, so the seam between buttons stays visible in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode
